skill.name: revise
skill.description: Revise p11 shareable documents from reviewer comments, apply clear decisions, and publish a new version with updated links.
skill.summary: Apply clear p11 review comments and publish a revised shareable document.
skill.author: rarexlabs

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives after manual review. The confirmed risks are legitimate external p11 CLI execution, global npm installation guidance, and networked document publishing, so publication is reasonable with a warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands and Global Package Install
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run p11 CLI commands and install or upgrade @p11-core/cli globally when needed. This is legitimate for the workflow, but it executes external tooling and depends on the latest npm package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly call command -v, npm install -g, p11 comments, p11 docs, and p11 share. The commands are purpose-aligned, but global installation and latest-version execution create supply-chain and environment-change risk.

medium_findings.title: Networked Document Fetching and Publishing
medium_findings.description: The skill fetches review comments and publishes a new version of a p11 document through the p11 CLI. This is expected behavior, but it can transmit document content and requires care with edit URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file uses the words fetch, publish, share, and edit URL around p11 CLI commands. No malicious endpoint is shown, but the workflow clearly involves external p11 services and sensitive document links.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts are false positives. The cited lines contain a display description or component names, and no cryptographic operation or algorithm choice is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, random generation, or cryptographic API usage at these locations. The matches appear to come from ordinary text substrings.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance alert is a false positive. The cited line tells the assistant to return p11 URLs and does not gather host, user, process, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains output guidance for updated document links. I found no evidence of reconnaissance commands or system inventory collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Commands and Global Package Install
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run p11 CLI commands and install or upgrade @p11-core/cli globally when needed. This is legitimate for the workflow, but it executes external tooling and depends on the latest npm package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly call command -v, npm install -g, p11 comments, p11 docs, and p11 share. The commands are purpose-aligned, but global installation and latest-version execution create supply-chain and environment-change risk.

content.user_title: Revise p11 Documents From Review Comments
content.value_statement: Review comments can be hard to convert into a clean document revision. This skill gathers clear p11 feedback, applies it to the source document, and publishes an updated version.
actual_capabilities: Resolve a p11 target from a URL, ID, source file, chat history, or p11 history.
Fetch visible p11 comments and replies before editing the source document.
Separate clear reviewer decisions from unresolved or conflicting feedback.
Apply clear revisions to static p11 document components.
Publish a revised version with p11 share and return available document links.
Use p11 component guidance to keep documents reviewable and non-interactive.
limitations: Requires access to the p11 CLI or permission to install it globally.
Does not decide unresolved reviewer disagreements without user direction.
Only supports p11 document revisions, not general web application editing.
May expose document content to p11 services during comments and share operations.

use_cases.title: Update a proposal after review
use_cases.description: Apply accepted comments to a p11 proposal and publish a new version for stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Revise technical documentation
use_cases.description: Convert reviewer decisions into static p11 document edits while preserving supported components.

use_cases.title: Prepare final review artifacts
use_cases.description: Resolve clear feedback, skip undecided threads by request, and return updated read and edit links.

prompt_templates.title: Revise latest document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request using recent chat context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise on the most recent p11 document. Apply clear comments and ask me about any unclear threads.

prompt_templates.title: Revise a specific document
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a known p11 target.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise with this p11 edit URL. Fetch reviewer comments, apply clear decisions, and publish a new version.

prompt_templates.title: Use historical feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs comments from a specific version.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise for version 3 of this document. Apply only visible clear reviewer decisions from that version.

prompt_templates.title: Controlled revision workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with strict review handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise on this source file and edit URL. List unclear threads before editing, then revise only after my decision.

output_examples.input: Revise the latest p11 document from the current review comments.
output_examples.output: The document was revised from clear reviewer decisions. One unclear thread needs your decision before a further revision.

output_examples.input: Apply comments from this p11 edit URL and publish the update.
output_examples.output: A new version was published. The response includes the updated read link and keeps the edit link limited to the user.

output_examples.input: Use version 2 comments and ignore unresolved discussion.
output_examples.output: Only clear visible comments from version 2 were applied. Unresolved discussion was left unchanged by request.

best_practices: Provide the p11 edit URL and source file when possible.
Review unclear comment threads before publishing a revision.
Keep edit URLs private because they can grant document modification access.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-p11 documents or general application pages.
Do not publish when visible reviewer comments still need clarification.
Do not add interactive controls or app-shell components to a p11 document.

faq.question: What does this skill revise?
faq.answer: It revises p11 shareable documents based on visible reviewer comments and replies.

faq.question: Can it work without a p11 URL?
faq.answer: Yes, it can use recent chat context or p11 history when no target is provided.

faq.question: Does it handle unclear feedback?
faq.answer: It asks the user before applying unclear, conflicting, or undecided review threads.

faq.question: Does it publish automatically?
faq.answer: It publishes after clear decisions are applied and required targets are available.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires the p11 CLI, which the instructions may install or upgrade globally.

faq.question: Are edit URLs sensitive?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill treats edit URLs as bearer credentials and avoids unnecessary exposure.
